message:
Ever watched your dog twitching, paddling their paws and giving little muffled "woofs" in their sleep, and wondered what on earth is going on in that fuzzy head? 🐶💤 Chances are, you're watching your best friend dream.

Just like us, dogs drift into a deep stage of sleep called REM — and that's where the magic happens. During REM, the brain lights up with activity almost as though it were wide awake. You can usually spot it: eyes flickering behind closed lids, legs gently running, whiskers twitching, maybe a soft yip or a sigh. That's a dog deep in a dream.

So what are they dreaming about? Scientists have a heart-melting answer. Studies of sleeping brains suggest animals tend to replay the events of their day — which means your dog is most likely dreaming about the things they love best. Chasing squirrels, racing across the yard, sniffing out that one incredible smell on a walk… and, because dogs are so deeply bonded to their people, very probably about you. 💛

In one famous experiment, researchers gently switched off the part of the brain that normally keeps the body still during sleep — and the dogs began acting out their dreams, running, sniffing and play-hunting right there on the floor. Proof that those twitchy little paws really are chasing something wonderful in dreamland.

So tonight, when your pup starts paddling away in their sleep, just picture the happy little adventure they're on — quite possibly with their favorite human running right alongside them. 🥰

What does YOUR dog do in their sleep — the running paws, the muffled barks, or the full-on dream "woofs"? Tell me below! 🐾
